content:
奇怪的词不要乱用啊！！
the MTL says "Don't use strange words carelessly!" (exasperated at Casiah being Casiah)
is there a better way to translate this
虽然朵尔老师确实很辛苦，但唯独不想被你嫌弃啊！
same with this one (Though Ms. Doll is indeed hardworking, I especially don't want to be disliked by you!)

hmm, so looking at this convo in context, it's kind of a whole mess...notes on the entire thing:
> What are you up to again??
should be "What are you up to this time??"
> It may be a bit hard to understand, but from now on, please call me Teacher Casiah!
> Uh, Casiah... Professor. I understand even less than you do!!
this whole exchange is weird in english because chinese omits the subject, but the first line is basically casiah saying she doesn't quite get (what it means to be a teacher), and the keeper responding with you don't get it?? i'm the one who doesn't get how you can be a teacher!!!
> Things are getting more and more off, aren't they!!
dunno if it came through well but casiah lists 3 things she's teaching and they get more and more suspicious as it goes on (first is grabbing things out of midair like in juggling/throwing, which is pretty normal. next is a martial arts terminology meaning hitting something through something else, like inner force/penetration, which is a bit weird but ok. last is just straight up stealing shit out of a safe at which point it's like ayo what the fuck)
> ...You can't learn these!
this works terribly in english because the sentence structure doesn't work, but the previous line that ends in ... and this line that starts with ... actually form a single sentence, but because of the way it splits up, it first sounds like she's listing things she's going to teach you, before the sentence finishes where it turns out she's actually listing things she WON'T teach you. it flows naturally in chinese but sounds awkward in english because english sentence structure doesn't really lend itself to this word order where the target comes before the verb

ok after that it goes into the line you're referring to:
> 奇怪的词不要乱用啊！！
where she's saying "stop throwing out weird phrases!!", because she's responding to casiah saying she'll teach you 溜门撬锁 and 顺手牵羊
chinese has 4-word idioms which are, well, idioms comprised of 4 words and are usually some metaphorical saying with a common meaning
溜门撬锁 means sneaking in and picking locks (i.e., breaking an entering) - afaik this isn't actually one but the way it's structured makes it sound like one
顺手牵羊 is actually an idiom from the 36 strategems but it's not actually used that way in modern convo, in modern culture it just means stealing shit

but anyway in this context "weird" means "socially unacceptable", so basically casiah is like, trying to sound fancy with idioms but she's actually saying i'll teach you how to commit crime and the keeper is like don't teach people how to do crime!!!!
......man I'm not sure if i'm explaining it well
yeah Casiahs texts are just
wacky
but basically Casiah is being a smartass and Keeper is tsukomiing or whatever

> 虽然朵尔老师确实很辛苦，但唯独不想被你嫌弃啊！
this is like, uhhh

the line before it basically going "wow, does doll always have to deal with students this troublesome? tch, being a teacher is so annoying"
and the keeper goes "yeah doll does have it pretty tough sometimes but one thing I don't want to be is to be disliked by you!!!" as in like, acknowledging that yeah doll does have to deal with annoying students but this isn't that and of all people, i don't wanna hear it from you
anyway yeah these texts are uhhhhhhh
good luck
yeah I'll do my best...
this is the kind of mess i'd spend like an hour trying to figure out how to word before giving up
you can do it
damn you're in my walls
well I think I made something that's "fine" at least for now
